meanings.definition: Having thick, hard skin formed because of repeated work, pressure, or friction, especially on the hands or feet.
examples: The farmer had rough, callused hands from years of labor.
Her feet became callused after walking long distances barefoot.
The carpenter’s callused fingers showed signs of hard work.
He gently touched the guitar strings with his callused fingertips.

meanings.definition: To develop thick and hardened skin because of constant rubbing, pressure, or physical work.
examples: His palms callused after weeks of lifting heavy weights.
Years of rowing had callused her hands.
The skin slowly callused from repeated friction.
Working with tools every day had callused his fingers.

meanings.definition: A complete list of items, books, products, or information arranged in an organized way.
examples: She looked through the clothing catalogue before ordering.
The library catalogue helped students find books easily.
The company released a new catalogue of products.
He searched the online catalogue for old movies.

meanings.definition: To make an organized list of items, information, or collections.
examples: The museum carefully catalogued its ancient artifacts.
She catalogued all the books in her study room.
Scientists catalogued different species found in the forest.
The librarian spent the afternoon cataloguing new arrivals.
